D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

HDR Colour on the Web

CSS color() opens P3 and Rec. 2020 to the page, while a brightness headroom above SDR white lets highlights truly glow

HDR on the web has two axes. Gamut: color() can address sRGB, display-p3, or rec2020, each a wider triangle of chromaticities. Brightness: an HDR display has headroom above the SDR reference white (~203 nits), so highlights can be set far brighter than diffuse white instead of clipping at 1.0.

Switch the colour space to grow the gamut triangle, and raise the HDR headroom to lift the peak above SDR white. Together they are what "HDR content" means: more colours, and more light at the top end.
